zh.lifeSummary: 當Frederick Elsick於1500年出生在英國素瑞霍利時，他的父親Frederick Elsick31歲，母親Jane Joane Kesteven DICKENS23歲。他於1519年與Mary Hackney在英國素可霍沙安結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1590年之前在他的家鄉，埋葬在英國英格蘭素瑞萊蓋特 安德 班斯亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Frederick Elsick于1500年出生在英国素瑞霍利时，他的父亲Frederick Elsick31岁，母亲Jane Joane Kesteven DICKENS23岁。他于1519年与Mary Hackney在英国素可霍沙安结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1590年之前在他的家乡，埋葬在英国英格兰素瑞萊蓋特 安德 班斯亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Elsick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Serbian and Croatian Ilić (see Ilic ) and possibly also of Jelić (see Yelich ). Compare Elick .

variants.name: Illig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Dieter, Ernst, Otto, Reinhold, Siegbert.
German: from a vernacular form of a medieval personal name, the Latin form of which was Aegidius (see Giles ).

nameMeanings.name: Frederick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Fredrick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Fredric
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

en.lifeSummary: When Frederick Elsick was born in 1500, in Horley, Surrey, England, his father, Frederick Elsick, was 31 and his mother, Jane Joane Kesteven DICKENS, was 23. He married Mary Hackney in 1519, in Horsham, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died before 1590, in his hometown, and was buried in Reigate and Banstead, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Mary Hackney en 1519, à Horsham, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé avant 1590, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Reigate and Banstead, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Frederick Elsick(이)가 1500년 영국 수리 호를리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Frederick Elsick(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Joane Kesteven DICKENS(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1519년 영국 수세스 호르샤에서 Mary Hackney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1590년 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수리 레익앳 앤드 반스테아드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Frederick Elsick родился в 1500 г. в местности хаурли, сурри, Англия, его отцу по имени Frederick Elsick было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Jane Joane Kesteven DICKENS, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Hackney в 1519 г. в местности хауршам, суссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер перед 1590 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности ригэит анд банстид, сурри, Англия, Великобритания.
